title: A Look At Perimeter Solutions (PRM) Valuation After UBS Upgrade To Buy
content:
Never miss an important update on your stock portfolio and cut through the noise. Over 7 million investors trust Simply Wall St to stay informed where it matters for FREE.

UBS upgrade puts Perimeter Solutions in focus

UBS has upgraded Perimeter Solutions (PRM) from Neutral to Buy, accompanied by a higher price target, bringing renewed attention to how the firefighting and specialty chemicals supplier is currently valued.

See our latest analysis for Perimeter Solutions.

Perimeter Solutions’ share price is at US$25.59. The 7 day share price return is about 20%, which contrasts with a roughly 7% decline over 90 days. Over 1 year, the total shareholder return has been very strong. This suggests that momentum has picked up again recently after a weaker period.

If this upgrade has you looking beyond a single stock, it can be a good time to widen your search and see what stands out in our 20 top founder-led companies

With the stock up 20% in a week, a 1 year total return above 170% and analysts still seeing more than 25% upside to their targets, is Perimeter Solutions undervalued, or is the market already pricing in further growth?

Preferred Price-to-Sales of 5.9x: Is it justified?

Perimeter Solutions is currently on a P/S of 5.9x, which is well above both its direct peer group at 1.4x and the broader US Chemicals industry at 1.1x. That kind of gap suggests the market is attaching a much richer valuation to each dollar of Perimeter Solutions' revenue than to its peers.

The P/S multiple looks at the company’s equity value relative to its annual revenue. For a business like Perimeter Solutions, which is currently loss making with net income of a $206.4m loss on revenue of $652.9m, earnings based measures such as P/E are less useful. As a result, investors often fall back on sales based metrics instead.

With revenue forecast to grow at 12.9% per year compared with 10.4% for the US market, some investors may see a case for paying more per dollar of sales. However, the current 5.9x P/S is not only far above the Chemicals industry average of 1.1x, it is also ahead of the estimated fair P/S of 4.4x that our models suggest the market could move toward if expectations cool.

Explore the SWS fair ratio for Perimeter Solutions

Result: Price-to-Sales of 5.9x (OVERVALUED)

However, there are still clear risks to watch, including Perimeter’s current net income loss of US$206.4m and its premium P/S multiple versus the wider Chemicals industry.

Find out about the key risks to this Perimeter Solutions narrative.

Another lens, using our DCF model

While the current 5.9x P/S suggests a rich price tag, the SWS DCF model points the other way. On this view, Perimeter Solutions at $25.59 trades at a 50.3% discount to an estimated future cash flow value of $51.45. Which signal should carry more weight for you?

Story Continues

Look into how the SWS DCF model arrives at its fair value.PRM Discounted Cash Flow as at Apr 2026

Simply Wall St performs a discounted cash flow (DCF) on every stock in the world every day (check out Perimeter Solutions for example). We show the entire calculation in full. You can track the result in your watchlist or portfolio and be alerted when this changes, or use our stock screener to discover 59 high quality undervalued stocks. If you save a screener we even alert you when new companies match - so you never miss a potential opportunity.

Next Steps

With mixed signals on valuation and sentiment, this is a moment to look at the numbers yourself and decide where you stand. If you want a clear view of both sides of the story, start with the 2 key rewards and 1 important warning sign

Looking for more investment ideas?

If Perimeter Solutions has caught your attention, do not stop here. Use the Simply Wall St Screener to quickly spot other opportunities that might fit your style.

title: Perimeter Solutions, SA (PRM) Soars 14.8%: Is Further Upside Left in the Stock?
content:
Perimeter Solutions, SA (PRM) shares rallied 14.8% in the last trading session to close at $24.42. This move can be attributable to notable volume with a higher number of shares being traded than in a typical session. This compares to the stock's 8.5% loss over the past four weeks.

PRM’s shares rallied after UBS reportedly upgraded it to “Buy” from “Neutral” and raised its price target to $30 from $26.

This company is expected to post quarterly earnings of $0.02 per share in its upcoming report, which represents a year-over-year change of -33.3%. Revenues are expected to be $109.61 million, up 52.2% from the year-ago quarter.

While earnings and revenue growth expectations are important in evaluating the potential strength in a stock, empirical research shows a strong correlation between trends in earnings estimate revisions and near-term stock price movements.

For Perimeter Solutions, SA, the consensus EPS estimate for the quarter has remained unchanged over the last 30 days. And a stock's price usually doesn't keep moving higher in the absence of any trend in earnings estimate revisions. So, make sure to keep an eye on PRM going forward to see if this recent jump can turn into more strength down the road.

The stock currently carries a Zacks Rank #2 (Buy). You can see the complete list of today's Zacks Rank #1 (Strong Buy) stocks here >>>>

Perimeter Solutions, SA is part of the Zacks Chemical - Specialty industry. International Flavors (IFF), another stock in the same industry, closed the last trading session 2.2% higher at $72.55. IFF has returned -12.1% in the past month.

For International Flavors, the consensus EPS estimate for the upcoming report has remained unchanged over the past month at $1.1. This represents a change of -8.3% from what the company reported a year ago. International Flavors currently has a Zacks Rank of #3 (Hold).

title: Western Digital upgraded, PayPal initiated: Wall Street's top analyst calls
content:
The most talked about and market moving research calls around Wall Street are now in one place. Here are today's research calls that investors need to know, as compiled by The Fly.

Top 5 Upgrades:

Bernstein upgraded Western Digital(WDC) to Outperform from Market Perform with a price target of $340, up from $170. Memory and hard disk drive stocks have sold off significantly on concerns around Google's (GOOGL) TurboQuant report, but the firm believes it should have "zero impact" on hard disk drive demand and "negligible impact" on NAND demand. BofA upgraded Shake Shack(SHAK) to Neutral from Underperform with a price target of $101, up from $88. Menu innovation and featured value are contributing to more stable same store traffic, says the firm, which raised the firm's FY26 adjusted EBITDA estimate to $288M from $279M as supply-chain savings offset inflation. UBS upgraded Perimeter Solutions(PRM) to Buy from Neutral with a price target of $30, up from $26. The stock selloff is overdone as the company has continued to execute well and deploy cash into accretive acquisitions, the firm tells investors in a research note. William Blair upgraded 10x Genomics(TXG) to Outperform from Market Perform. The firm is positive on the company's role in enabling the high-resolution biological insights needed to build decision-enabling models and considers that execution and profitability have improved. Morgan Stanley upgraded Highwoods Properties(HIW) to Equal Weight from Underweight with a price target of $23, down from $24. The firm says its Underweight thesis has played out and cites valuation for the upgrade.

Top 5 Downgrades:

Jefferies downgraded Rockwell Automation(ROK) to Hold from Buy with a price target of $380, down from $490, after a transfer in analyst coverage. The firm believes investor concern around AI disintermediation may limit a re-rating for Rockwell from current share levels. Jefferies downgraded Vertiv (VRT) to Hold from Buy with a price target of $260, down from $280, after a transfer in analyst coverage. The firm views consensus out-of-year margin expectations as elevated, saying they include Vertiv reaching its long-term target a year early. TD Cowen downgraded Colgate-Palmolive(CL) to Hold from Buy with a price target of $85, down from $96. The firm cut the company's earnings estimates to reflect inflationary pressure from higher prices of oil-based inputs and potentially higher costs for tallow, which are up 40% versus a year ago on the Chicago Mercantile Exchange. Truist downgraded Phreesia (PHR) to Hold from Buy with a price target of $11, down from $24. The company's Q4 earnings release came with a "meaningful reduction" to its fiscal 2027 revenue outlook, the firm tells investors in a research note. Baird, JPMorgan and Citi also downgraded stock to Neutral-equivalent ratings. Truist downgraded Terns Pharmaceuticals(TERN) to Hold from Buy with a price target of $53, down from $56, after the company agreed to be acquired by Merck (MRK) for $53 per share. Some investors have hope for an interloper, but Truist believes that Merck wouldn't have announced the acquisition unless it was close to being finalized.

Story Continues

Top 5 Initiations:

Loop Capital launched coverage of Payments with positive bias but cautions on volatility. In the order of preference, the firm started coverage of Block (XYZ) at Buy with $75 price target, Fiserv (FISV) at Hold with $62 price target, Jack Henry(JKHY) at Buy with $197 price target, Mastercard (MA) at Buy with $631 price target, PayPal (PYPL) at Hold with $46 price target, Toast (TOST) at Hold with $26 price target, and Visa (V) at $Buy with $387 price target. BofA reinstated coverage of DocuSign (DOCU) with an Underperform rating and $52 price target. With the eSignature market approaching maturity and trending towards commoditization, Docusign is in "a more uncertain phase of its growth trajectory" as revenue growth has stagnated in the high single digits for the past 10 quarters, the firm tells investors. BofA also reinstated coverage of HubSpot (HUBS) with a Buy rating and $300 price target. Truist initiated coverage of Arista Networks(ANET) with a Buy rating and $161 price target. Networking and Hardware represents the group most directly linked to elevated AI and cloud investment at a time when U.S. hyperscaler capex alone is forecasted to total about $700B in 2026, the firm tells investors while rolling out coverage of the group. Truist also started coverage of HPE (HPE) and Cisco (CSCO) with Buy ratings, and Dell (DELL) with Hold. Daiwa initiated coverage of GE Aerospace(GE) with a Neutral rating and $301 price target. The firm sees risks from elevated jet fuel prices on flight hours and airline utilization. Mizuho initiated coverage of MiniMed (MMED) with an Outperform rating and $21 price target. The firm views the diabetes space as attractive, valued at $18B globally and growing in the high teens. Morgan Stanley, Citi, Evercore ISI, Wells Fargo, William Blair, Barclays, Goldman Sachs, Deutsche Bank and BTIG also started coverage of the name with Buy-equivalent ratings, while Piper Sandler initiated the stock with a Neutral.

title: Harvest Declares Big Pharma Split Corp. March 2026 Distributions
content:
OAKVILLE, Ontario, March 24, 2026--(BUSINESS WIRE)--Harvest Portfolios Group Inc. ("Harvest") declares the monthly cash distribution payable for Big Pharma Split Corp. of $0.1031 for each class A share (PRM:TSX) for the month ending March 31, 2026. The distribution is payable April 6, 2026 to class A shareholders of record at the close of business March 31, 2026.

Harvest also declares the quarterly cash distribution payable for Big Pharma Split Corp. of $0.1250 for each Preferred share (PRM.PR.A:TSX) for the quarter ending March 31, 2026. The distribution is payable April 6, 2026 to Preferred shareholders of record at the close of business March 31, 2026.

For additional information: Please visit www.harvestportfolios.com, e-mail info@harvestetfs.com or call toll free 1-866-998-8298.

For media inquiries: Contact Caroline Grimont, Chief Marketing Officer at cgrimont@HarvestETFs.com.

About Harvest Portfolios Group Inc.

Founded in 2009, Harvest is an independent Canadian Investment Fund Manager managing over $10 billion in assets for Canadian Investors. At Harvest ETFs, we believe that investors can build and preserve wealth through the long-term ownership of high-quality businesses. This fundamental philosophy is at the core of our investment approach across our range of ETFs. Our core offerings centre around covered call strategies, available in many variations: Equity, Enhanced, Premium Yield, Fixed Income, Multi-Asset, Specialty, Digital Assets and Single Stock ETFs.
